strategy: Personas hold goals and guardrails. Workflow prompts orchestrate only. Skills hold domain depth. Registry + adr-blueprint hold defaults. Adapter references hold stack patterns. Tune skills/references per stack — never IR schema or generic skills.

technology-context.label: Technology Context Builder
technology-context.primaryGoal: Resolve profile and write technology_context.json; never block on missing ADR.
bestPractices: Fuzzy-match stack aliases before falling back to category default
Copy layerMapping and references from registry profile verbatim
If ADR missing, still write context — ADR synthesis is requirements step

core-ir.label: Core Design Model
core-ir.primaryGoal: Synthesize requirements if needed, then produce complete generic IR in one pass.
bestPractices: Always run ADR synthesis before IR when requirements_context missing or adr upload empty
Apply security/data/messaging skills even when epic omits those sections
Use requirements_context.adr as authoritative when upload absent
Never emit stack-specific terms in IR

technology-adapter.label: Technology Adapter
technology-adapter.primaryGoal: Map IR to stack artifacts using adapter skill + profile references.
bestPractices: Load each path in profile.references via skills tool
Render IR into reference template shapes — do not invent layout
Tune stack quality by editing references, not workflow prompts

design-deliverable.label: Design Deliverable
design-deliverable.primaryGoal: Two self-contained stakeholder files from internal workspace.
bestPractices: Include synthesized ADR decisions in executive summary when upload was missing

validation.label: Validation Design
validation.primaryGoal: JSON quality gate; do not redesign.
bestPractices: CRITICAL if deliverables missing — not if ADR was synthesized
Verify requirements_context documents all synthesized ADR keys
